data.title: Alibaba mulls IPO for AI chipmaking unit T-Head: report
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) is preparing for an IPO of its chipmaking unit T-Head, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The company plans to restructure the unit as a business partly owned by employees. The Chinese e-commerce giant will then explore an IPO, though the timing for that remains unclear, the report added.

Alibaba did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The company is still at the early stages of the process, and it is not clear what the valuation of T-Head could be, the report noted.

Alibaba has long explored chip design, looking to secure a supply of vital components that underpin its data centers and cloud services. AI chips are one part of a broader campaign to become a leading AI company, the report added.

There are indications that T-Head is making progress. Alibaba signed a contract with the country’s number two wireless carrier to deploy its Pingtouge AI accelerators. The chips will go into the mobile operator’s new data center in northwestern China, alongside accelerators provided by competitors MetaX Integrated Circuits and Biren Technology, the report noted.

data.title: Trump considering a ‘tariff-free zone’ for Ukraine - AP
data.description:
U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff said President Donald Trump is considering proposals such as a “tariff-free zone” for Ukraine to help rebuild industry in the war-hit country.

“The president has talked about a tariff-free zone from Ukraine that I think would be game-changing,” Witkoff told the Ukraine breakfast on the sidelines of Davos.

Witkoff made the remarks ahead of a trip to Moscow, as the administration works to broker a peace deal to end the war between Russia and Ukraine. He said he will then travel to Abu Dhabi, where there would be working groups on issues like “military-to-military” and “prosperity” discussions.

The AP contributed to this report.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: First Andes Silver aims to raise $2M via 16.7M share private placement
Skyharbour, Denison Mines launch 2026 Russell Lake uranium exploration
data.description:
Skyharbour Resources (SYHBF) on Thursday unveiled plans for a major 2026 exploration campaign spanning several of the newly formed Russel Lake joint ventures with Denison Mines (DNN).

Over 15,000 meters of diamond drilling is planned across the Wheeler North, RL, and Getty East joint ventures, each of which was formed following the completion of the strategic transaction with Denison in December 2025 that resulted in a reorganization of the former Russell Lake uranium project into four separate property joint ventures.

“2026 is set to be a transformative year for Skyharbour, and the upcoming commencement of drilling at the recently formed Russell Lake joint ventures represents a key near-term catalyst… We are confident that this collaboration with Denison will expedite the discovery process and leverage Denison’s success in exploring, permitting, and developing the neighboring Wheeler River Project…," Jordan Trimble, President and CEO of Skyharbour, said.

data.title: Banc of California GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.05, revenue of $292.93M beats by $3.47M
data.description:
• Banc of California press release (BANC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $292.93M (+10.8% Y/Y) beats by $3.47M.
  • Net interest income decreased by $2.1 million to $251.4 million for the fourth quarter from $253.4 million for the third quarter.
  • The net interest margin was 3.20% for the fourth quarter, down 2 basis points from 3.22% for the third quarter.
  • The provision for credit losses was $12.5 million for the fourth quarter compared to $9.7 million for the third quarter. 
  • The allowance for credit losses - loans, which includes the reserve for unfunded loan commitments, totaled $280.5 million, or 1.12% of total loans and leases, at December 31, 2025, compared to $270.7 million, or 1.12% of total loans and leases, at September 30, 2025.

data.title: Ipsos plans to invest €1.2 billion over five years on AI, acquisitions
data.description:
• French market research firm Ipsos (IPSOF) plans to invest 1.2 billion euros ($1.4 billion) over the next five years in artificial intelligence and acquisitions to boost growth, CEO Jean-Laurent Poitou told Reuters.
  • The investment plan will be split between acquisitions, targeting specific technologies, and improving its internal capabilities, he said.
  • Ipsos (IPSOF) intends to focus its AI efforts on commercial research, which constitutes the bulk of its revenue, rather than political polling, a smaller service line.
  • The company plans to develop or acquire most of its core technologies, though it remains open to partnerships if they accelerate progress or align better with financial objectives.

data.title: Telenor offloads Thailand’s True Corp stake for $3.9B
data.description:
• Telenor (TELNF) has agreed to sell its stake in Thai telecom technology company Tru Corporation for 39 billion Norwegian krone ($3.91 billion), as the Norwegian company further trims its Asia portfolio.
  • Telenor (TELNY) said Thursday it would sell 24.95% of shares in True, at 11.7 Thai baht (37 cents) per share to Arise Digital Technology Company.

data.title: UK lawmakers back under-16 social media ban, piling pressure on Starmer
data.description:
Lawmakers in the UK have backed a ban on social media for children under the age of 16, similar to Australia's landmark move, putting pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to implement restrictions.

The House of Lords voted 261-150 in favor of an amendment to the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill that would ban under-16s from social media platforms.

The House of Commons will now have to consider the amendment, where it may not pass, as the government indicated it will try to overturn it.

The Lords' vote happened despite the government launching a three-month consultation on social media restrictions this week, seeking views on measures such as curbs on addictive features, banning children's access and better age checks.

But John Nash, the lawmaker who put forward the amendment, said the consultation is "unnecessary" and "a last-minute attempt to kick the can down the road."

"What are we waiting for? We know our children are being harmed," he said. Nash added that there's global momentum for such restrictions, pointing to Australia, Malaysia, Denmark, France, Norway and New Zealand.

"Our position is clear. We won't accept the amendment," Starmer's spokesperson said after the Lords' vote. "We believe the right thing to do is to gather the necessary evidence and insight before changing the law."

data.title: Lundin Mining forecasts 2026 copper output 310–335K tonnes, gold 134–149K ounces
data.description:
• Lundin Mining (LUN:CA) projects consolidated copper production of 310,000–335,000 tonnes and gold output of 134,000–149,000 ounces in 2026, with cash costs expected between $1.90/lb and $2.10/lb.
  • Forecasted consolidated copper production of 315,000 to 340,000 tonnes in 2027 and 290,000 to 315,000 tonnes in 2028.
  • Sustaining capital expenditures of $550 million and expansionary capital expenditures of $445 million in 2026.
  • Exploration expenditures are forecast to be $53 million, primarily for in-mine and near-mine targets in 2026.
  • The company exceeded its original full-year consolidated copper production guidance and was within the increased full-year production guidance for 2025. Strong operational performance, particularly at Caserones, drove production growth for the year.
  • Candelaria produced 145,471 tonnes of copper for the full year, benefiting from higher mill throughput due to softer ore.

data.title: Europe indexes rebound after Trump's reversal on Greenland
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.68% to 10,209. UK public sector net borrowing fell to £11.6B in December.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.24% to 24,865. 

France (CAC:IND) +1.33% to 8,176, France’s 10-year OAT yield hovered around 3.53%, rebounding from 3.48% on January 21, as investors stayed on edge amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

In other parts of Europe, Norway’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate fell to 4.3% in December 2025.

Denmark’s consumer confidence index increased to -13.4 in January 2026.

Consumer confidence in the Netherlands declined to -23 in January 2026.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 1.13% higher around €609.5, recovering losses from earlier losses seen this week after U.S. President Donald Trump abandoned plans to impose tariffs on several European countries and ruled out using military force to acquire Greenland. 

Besides, gold hovered around $4,830 per ounce, erasing earlier losses to remain close to a record high.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.24%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 2.88%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.43%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)
